clauses.statement: safeReaddir returns the sorted entries of a directory, returns an empty array for both ENOENT (missing path) and ENOTDIR (a file where a directory was expected), and re-throws any other error.
safeReaddir propagates errors whose code is neither ENOENT nor ENOTDIR instead of swallowing them.
buildEvidenceItem makes the explicit kind and ref arguments win over any colliding key in the extra bag, so a stray extra.kind or extra.ref cannot override the caller's intended identity.

buildEvidenceItem applies the defaults declared/strength-not-classified/n-a/unknown when extra omits strength, strength_reason, binding_status, or artifact_quality, and preserves those fields when extra provides them.

buildEvidenceItem carries through descriptive extra fields (for example matched_file_count, related_file_count, investigation_files, optional) while keeping the correct kind and defaults, and buildDocumentationEvidence's add helper no longer needs to duplicate kind inside extra.

The execution-state scanning workflow, when reading a pr or audit directory via safeReaddir, transitions to the 'continue with no entries' state for both the ENOENT (missing) and ENOTDIR (malformed: file where a directory was expected) outcomes instead of transitioning to an abort/throw state, so the pr-prepare and reconcile workflow stays resilient. Any other error transitions to the re-throw state.

diagrams.title: safeReaddir error classification
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Call["safeReaddir(dir)"] --> Try{"readdir succeeds?"}
  Try -->|yes| Sorted["return sorted entries"]
  Try -->|ENOENT / ENOTDIR| Empty["return []"]
  Try -->|other error| Throw["re-throw"]

diagrams.title: buildEvidenceItem field precedence
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Extra["...extra (descriptive fields)"] --> Merge["merged object"]
  Explicit["kind / ref / defaulted fields"] --> Merge
  Merge --> Win["explicit + defaults win over colliding extra keys"]
  Win --> Item["evidence item with intact identity + carried-through extras"]

diagrams.title: Malformed workspace and evidence-identity threats
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Malformed["malformed workspace: file where a directory is expected"] --> Scan["execution-state scanning (safeReaddir)"]
  Scan --> Control1["ENOTDIR treated as no-entries: workflow continues, not aborts"]
  Collision["extra bag carrying a stray kind/ref"] --> Assemble["buildEvidenceItem"]
  Assemble --> Control2["explicit kind/ref win: evidence identity cannot be hijacked"]
  Control1 --> Safe["pr-prepare/reconcile stays correct on edge inputs"]
  Control2 --> Safe
